final_answer:
Implemented: the `>2/3` validator approval flow now uses snapshot-based voter eligibility for active nonces, while keeping live current-validator gating only for opening a new proposal.

Why this matters for Gold Chain: validator-set churn can no longer block valid snapshot voters mid-round, so admission stays deterministic and cannot be deadlocked by live-set drift.

What I changed:
1. Updated approval vote path in [validator_lifecycle.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/validator_lifecycle.go#L144):
   - removed live voter lookup for ongoing nonce votes,
   - kept live-set check only on `isNewProposal`,
   - added snapshot voter resolution helper `snapshotVoterValIDForOperator` at [validator_lifecycle.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/validator_lifecycle.go#L708),
   - used snapshot-resolved voter id for vote key + replay sequence.
2. Added regression tests in [msg_server_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/msg_server_test.go#L195):
   - `TestNativeValidatorApprovalSnapshotVoterCanVoteAfterSetDrift`
   - `TestNativeValidatorApprovalRejectsPostSnapshotValidator`
   - `TestNativeValidatorApprovalFinalizesUnderValidatorSetDrift`
   - plus helper `overwriteNativeValidatorSet` at [msg_server_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/msg_server_test.go#L485) to avoid duplicate setup code.

Checks run:
1. `go test ./x/stake/keeper -run 'TestKeeperTestSuite/TestNativeValidatorApproval' -count=1 -v` from `bridge/gilt-consensus`: pass.
2. `go test ./x/stake/... -count=1` from `bridge/gilt-consensus`: pass.
3. Initial test run from repo root failed because module root is `bridge/gilt-consensus`; rerun from correct module dir fixed it.

No partial gaps found in the requested scope after these checks.
